revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11419962]] Wikidata name: Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Artificial caves in Usa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =四日市横穴群 |alternate_name = |image = |alt = |caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = | notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster'''|四日市横穴群|Yokkaichi yokoana-gun}} is the collective name for two clusters of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} tombs dug in artificial caves in {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} cliffs in the Yokkaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217849 |title=四日市横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== In the latter half of the [[Kofun period]], the class of people buried expanded, and mass cemeteries in which a side hole is dug into the slope of a hill to provide a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], began to replace [[Kofun]]s. Such cemeteries could contain dozens to hundreds of tombs, with each tomb containing multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster consist of 161 horizontal cave tombs built into the tuff bedrock on the east slope of the Dainohara Hills. The necropolis extends for approximately 300 meters, These are divided into the {{nihongo|'''Kagayama Cluster'''|加賀山支群||}}, which has 75 cave tombs spread over an area of about 160 meters from north-to-south, arranged in six steps on a slope with an altitude of 28 to 40 meters, arranged in a staircase pattern, and the {{nihongo|'''Ikkite cluster'''|一鬼手支群||}}, with 86 cave tombs built on a slope of 28 to 36 meters above sea level, on a 3- to 4-tiered surface. Each of the cave tombs is oval in plan, with a semicylindrical burial chamber with a short passageway, with the ceiling gradually lowering towards the back. Many of the tombs have been open since antiquity, and since no systematic [[archaeological excavation]] has ever been performed, details of the burials and [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s are unknown. Some of the tombs are decorated with colored patterns, notably as Kagayama No.39, and No.40, and Ikkite No.62. The Kagayama decorations include four images of people standing with their hands outstretched, as well as concentric circles, in red with a black border. Ikkite No.62 has14 large and small red concentric circles.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is estimated that these tombs was built between the late 6th century and the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://fc.usa-city.jp/location/detail.php?cate=0&id=00202203210753352251 Usa city official webpage]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:四日市横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11419962]] Wikidata name: Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Artificial caves in Usa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =四日市横穴群 |alternate_name = |image = |alt = |caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = | notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster'''|四日市横穴群|Yokkaichi yokoana-gun}} is the collective name for two clusters of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} tombs dug in artificial caves in {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} cliffs in the Yokkaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217849 |title=四日市横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== In the latter half of the [[Kofun period]], the class of people buried expanded, and mass cemeteries in which a side hole is dug into the slope of a hill to provide a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], began to replace [[Kofun]]s. Such cemeteries could contain dozens to hundreds of tombs, with each tomb containing multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster consist of 161 horizontal cave tombs built into the tuff bedrock on the east slope of the Dainohara Hills. The necropolis extends for approximately 300 meters, These are divided into the {{nihongo|'''Kagayama Cluster'''|加賀山支群||}}, which has 75 cave tombs spread over an area of about 160 meters from north-to-south, arranged in six steps on a slope with an altitude of 28 to 40 meters, arranged in a staircase pattern, and the {{nihongo|'''Ikkite cluster'''|一鬼手支群||}}, with 86 cave tombs built on a slope of 28 to 36 meters above sea level, on a 3- to 4-tiered surface. Each of the cave tombs is oval in plan, with a semicylindrical burial chamber with a short passageway, with the ceiling gradually lowering towards the back. Many of the tombs have been open since antiquity, and since no systematic [[archaeological excavation]] has ever been performed, details of the burials and [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s are unknown. Some of the tombs are decorated with colored patterns, notably as Kagayama No.39, and No.40, and Ikkite No.62. The Kagayama decorations include four images of people standing with their hands outstretched, as well as concentric circles, in red with a black border. Ikkite No.62 has14 large and small red concentric circles.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is estimated that these tombs was built between the late 6th century and the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://fc.usa-city.jp/location/detail.php?cate=0&id=00202203210753352251 Usa city official webpage]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Artificial caves in Usa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster |native_name =四日市横穴群 |alternate_name = |image = |alt = |caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = | notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster'''|四日市横穴群|Yokkaichi yokoana-gun}} is the collective name for two clusters of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} tombs dug in artificial caves in {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} cliffs in the Yokkaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217849 |title=四日市横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== In the latter half of the [[Kofun period]], the class of people buried expanded, and mass cemeteries in which a side hole is dug into the slope of a hill to provide a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], began to replace [[Kofun]]s. Such cemeteries could contain dozens to hundreds of tombs, with each tomb containing multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster consist of 161 horizontal cave tombs built into the tuff bedrock on the east slope of the Dainohara Hills. The necropolis extends for approximately 300 meters, These are divided into the {{nihongo|'''Kagayama Cluster'''|加賀山支群||}}, which has 75 cave tombs spread over an area of about 160 meters from north-to-south, arranged in six steps on a slope with an altitude of 28 to 40 meters, arranged in a staircase pattern, and the {{nihongo|'''Ikkite cluster'''|一鬼手支群||}}, with 86 cave tombs built on a slope of 28 to 36 meters above sea level, on a 3- to 4-tiered surface. Each of the cave tombs is oval in plan, with a semicylindrical burial chamber with a short passageway, with the ceiling gradually lowering towards the back. Many of the tombs have been open since antiquity, and since no systematic [[archaeological excavation]] has ever been performed, details of the burials and [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s are unknown. Some of the tombs are decorated with colored patterns, notably as Kagayama No.39, and No.40, and Ikkite No.62. The Kagayama decorations include four images of people standing with their hands outstretched, as well as concentric circles, in red with a black border. Ikkite No.62 has14 large and small red concentric circles.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is estimated that these tombs was built between the late 6th century and the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://fc.usa-city.jp/location/detail.php?cate=0&id=00202203210753352251 Usa city official webpage]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Artificial caves in Usa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster |native_name =四日市横穴群 |alternate_name = |image = |alt = |caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E|display=inline,title}} | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = | notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|37|N|131|10|20|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster'''|四日市横穴群|Yokkaichi yokoana-gun}} is the collective name for two clusters of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} tombs dug in artificial caves in {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} cliffs in the Yokkaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa|1=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217849 |title=四日市横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== In the latter half of the [[Kofun period]], the class of people buried expanded, and mass cemeteries in which a side hole is dug into the slope of a hill to provide a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], began to replace [[Kofun]]s. Such cemeteries could contain dozens to hundreds of tombs, with each tomb containing multiple burials.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster consist of 161 horizontal cave tombs built into the tuff bedrock on the east slope of the Dainohara Hills. The necropolis extends for approximately 300 meters, These are divided into the {{nihongo|'''Kagayama Cluster'''|加賀山支群||}}, which has 75 cave tombs spread over an area of about 160 meters from north-to-south, arranged in six steps on a slope with an altitude of 28 to 40 meters, arranged in a staircase pattern, and the {{nihongo|'''Ikkite cluster'''|一鬼手支群||}}, with 86 cave tombs built on a slope of 28 to 36 meters above sea level, on a 3- to 4-tiered surface. Each of the cave tombs is oval in plan, with a semicylindrical burial chamber with a short passageway, with the ceiling gradually lowering towards the back. Many of the tombs have been open since antiquity, and since no systematic [[archaeological excavation]] has ever been performed, details of the burials and [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s are unknown. Some of the tombs are decorated with colored patterns, notably as Kagayama No.39, and No.40, and Ikkite No.62. The Kagayama decorations include four images of people standing with their hands outstretched, as well as concentric circles, in red with a black border. Ikkite No.62 has14 large and small red concentric circles.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is estimated that these tombs was built between the late 6th century and the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://fc.usa-city.jp/location/detail.php?cate=0&id=00202203210753352251 Usa city official webpage]{{in lang|ja}}
